sourceMovie.title: The Call
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Call for the same reason it broke through in 2020: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Timothy Woodward Jr. keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Call clicked for you, The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Call?
faq.answer: Try The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It, The Invisible Man, The Black Phone—each shares DNA with The Call while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Call?
faq.answer: The Call (2020) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Call worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
